channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Russia Pushes India for 100 Su-57 Deal with Local Production as IAF Weighs Rafale, Tejas Mk2, and AMCA Priorities
item.description: Russia has proposed that India commit to procuring at least 100 Su-57 fighters under a localized production model, accompanied by transfer of technology (ToT). The proposal goes beyond the Indian Air Force’s current interest, which is understood to be limited to a smaller government-to-government (G2G) deal for approximately 40 to 60 aircraft as an interim capability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Seeks Indigenous Long Range Surveillance Radars with 450 km Range and Advanced Drone Detection Capabilities
item.description: The Ministry of Defence (MoD) has initiated a fresh push toward strengthening India’s air surveillance and early warning network by inviting Indian vendors to participate in the development and supply of Long Range Surveillance Radars (LRSR). This move aligns with the broader “Aatmanirbhar Bharat” vision, aiming to reduce dependence on foreign radar systems while enhancing the Indian Armed Forces’ situational awareness across diverse operational environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL CATS Warrior Loyal Wingman to Receive Major AI Upgrade as MUM-T Architecture Evolves
item.description: India’s Combat Air Teaming System (CATS) Warrior loyal wingman program is set to receive significant upgrades in artificial intelligence and autonomy, according to information shared with idrw.org. Hindustan Aeronautics Limited is enhancing the Manned-Unmanned Teaming architecture to enable the Warrior drone to operate with greater independence, reflecting lessons drawn from recent global conflicts and rapidly evolving battlefield realities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s 99,000 Crore Submarine Deal with Germany to Include Strategic Inter-Government Agreement
item.description: India’s upcoming ?99,000 crore submarine deal for the indigenous construction of six advanced submarines at Mazagon Docks Limited (MDL) is set to be reinforced by a crucial Inter-Government Agreement (IGA) between New Delhi and Berlin. This development adds a sovereign guarantee layer to the contract, significantly elevating the strategic and contractual reliability of the deal with German industrial giant Thyssenkrupp Marine Systems (TKMS).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: SSS Defence’s Varaha C-UAS Achieves Breakthrough Milestone in Sustained Drone Localisation at 1 km Altitude
item.description: SSS Defence has once again demonstrated India’s growing prowess in advanced counter-drone technology as its flagship Varaha C-UAS system hits a significant operational milestone. The indigenous Counter-Unmanned Aircraft System successfully achieved sustained localisation of a 15 kg drone over a continuous four-minute period, tracking the target as it climbed to approximately one kilometre in altitude while maintaining an impressive error margin of less than three degrees.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Advances Work on 65,000-Tonne INS Vishal as DRDO Demonstrators Shape Future Naval Aviation
item.description: India’s next-generation aircraft carrier programme is quietly advancing as the Indian Navy and the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) continue work on the concept for the Indigenous Aircraft Carrier-III (IAC-III), widely referred to as INS Vishal in defence discussions. Even as the Navy awaits government clearance to build a second INS Vikrant-class carrier to maintain fleet strength, parallel technological progress is shaping a far more ambitious design philosophy for the future carrier.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Jayem Automotives Unveils Indigenous 180HP MALE UAV Engine at AeroDef India 2026 to Replace Imported Powerplants
item.description: India’s push toward indigenous aerospace propulsion received a significant boost as Jayem Automotives Pvt Ltd showcased its latest 180HP MALE (Medium Altitude Long Endurance) aero engine at the AeroDef India Manufacturing Expo 2026. The engine is positioned as a direct replacement for imported powerplants such as the Austro Engine E4 and Rotax series, which currently power key Indian UAV platforms including the TAPAS-BH-201 (Rustom-II) and Archer-NG.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders acquires Colombo Dockyard PLC (CDPLC) for 26.8 million
item.description: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL), India’s premier Defence Public Sector Undertaking under the Ministry of Defence, Government of India, announces that Colombo Dockyard PLC (CDPLC), Sri Lanka’s largest shipyard, has become a subsidiary of MDL following completion of the acquisition of a controlling 51% stake. MDL’s total investment is valued at USD 26.8 million.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Energy, defence and trade on agenda as Misri heads to Europe amid West Asia crisis
item.description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri will undertake a three-day visit to Paris and Berlin beginning Sunday for high-level talks on energy, trade and defence — sectors that have become increasingly critical amid a fractured geopolitical landscape.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Grenades Over Bombs: Inside ISI’s Shift To Low-Cost, High-Impact Terror In India
item.description: Intelligence agencies have flagged a shift in tactics by Pakistan’s spy agency, the Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI), with a growing reliance on low-cost, high-impact terror modules operating across India. Investigations have found that the use of solar-powered cameras for reconnaissance and grenades is the preferred weapon for attacks — marking a departure from more complex, resource-intensive operations involving improvised explosive devices (IEDs).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Ladakh finally gets distinct Aadhaar identity, ‘J&K’ tag removed
item.description: Nearly seven years after Ladakh was carved out as a separate Union Territory from the erstwhile state of Jammu and Kashmir in 2019, residents have finally secured a distinct identity in Aadhaar records, with the ‘Jammu and Kashmir’ label now replaced by ‘Ladakh’.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iran Struggles To Reopen Strait Of Hormuz As Missing Sea Mines Complicate Navigation: Report
item.description: A fresh complication has emerged in the already tense geopolitical landscape, with Iran reportedly facing difficulties in reopening the strategically crucial Strait of Hormuz after laying sea mines during recent hostilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, US to deepen nuclear ties, explore LPG exports
item.description: India’s Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri discussed furthering energy cooperation with the US in nuclear power and new areas such as coal gasification and LPG exports in his meeting with US Energy Secretary Chris Wright.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India-flagged LPG tanker Jag Vikram crosses Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: An India-flagged liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) tanker, Jag Vikram, has crossed the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first such transit by an Indian vessel since a temporary two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran was announced, according to ship-tracking data.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: INS Trikand Concludes Highly-Successful Mombasa Visit
item.description: INS Trikand, a frontline guided missile frigate of the Indian Navy, on Friday departed Kenya’s Mombasa following a highly successful Operational Turnaround (OTR) which included professional exchanges, community outreach, while strengthening maritime cooperation with the Kenyan Navy.
item.dc:creator: admin
